title: Minimize access to the approval sub-resource of certificatesigningrequests objects

input_row.title: Minimize access to the approval sub-resource of certificatesigningrequests objects
input_row.description: Users with access to the update the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects can approve new client certificates for the Kubernetes API effectively allowing them to create new high-privileged user accounts. This can allow for privilege escalation to full cluster administrator, depending on users configured in the cluster
input_row.rationale: The ability to update certificate signing requests should be limited.
input_row.audit: Review the users who have access to update the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects in the Kubernetes API.
input_row.remediation: Where possible, remove access to the approval sub-resource of CertificateSigningRequests objects.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_rbac_certificate_signing_requests_approval_access_restricted

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the RoleBindings and ClusterRoleBindings that grant access to the `approval` sub-resource of `CertificateSigningRequests` (CSRs).
- The specific API calls to use:
  - `kubectl get rolebindings --all-namespaces -o json` to retrieve all RoleBindings.
  - `kubectl get clusterrolebindings -o json` to retrieve all ClusterRoleBindings.
- Enumerate the Role and ClusterRole definitions to identify permissions related to `certificates.k8s.io` and specifically the `approval` verb on `certificatesigningrequests`.
- Check for any Role or ClusterRole that includes the following permissions:
  - `verbs: ["update"]`
  - `resource: ["certificatesigningrequests"]`
  - `subresource: ["approval"]`
- Clear Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **Pass:** No Role or ClusterRole grants the `update` permission on the `approval` sub-resource of `certificatesigningrequests`.
  - **Fail:** At least one Role or ClusterRole grants the `update` permission on the `approval` sub-resource of `certificatesigningrequests`.
